title: nerine marginata
intro: Nerine marginata, also known as the autumn-flowering nerine, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Amaryllidaceae family. It is native to South Africa and is known for its beautiful white or pink flowers. Traditionally, the Nerine marginata has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures to help alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory issues, as well as to promote wound healing and soothe digestive problems.
